'''Jason Baitieri''' (born 2 July 1989) is a [[France national rugby league team|France]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who retired professionally at the end of the 2021 season with the [[Catalans Dragons]] after 11 years with the club and over 250 appearances. Jason captained his country of birth [[France]] on multiple occasions including the 2017 [[Rugby League World Cup]] and the Inaugural [[Rugby League World Cup 9s]] competition.

He has previously played for the [[Sydney Roosters]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]], as well as [[bradford Bulls]] in the [[RFL Championship]]  . Jason is now head of Recruitment for the [[Baroudeurs de Pia XIII]] who play in the French [[FFR13]] Elite 1 competition

==Background==
Baitieri was born in [[Paris]], [[France]] to an Australian father [[Tas Baitieri|Tas]], a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer and coach, and a French mother.<ref>{{cite web|date=16 October 2017|title=Proud family to get behind Baiteri|url=https://culturalpulse.com.au/blog/2017/10/16/player-legend-profile-proud-family-to-get-behind-baiteri/|publisher=Cultural Pulse|access-date=25 March 2018|archive-date=1 April 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180401135321/https://culturalpulse.com.au/blog/2017/10/16/player-legend-profile-proud-family-to-get-behind-baiteri/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

He played his junior rugby league in [[Toulouse]] before returning to [[Castle Hill, New South Wales]] when he was 8, where he played for the [[Hills District Bulls]].<ref name=family-affair />

Baiteri was educated at [[Oakhill College|Oakhill College, Castle Hill]] and represented the [[Australian Schoolboys rugby league team|2007 Australian Schoolboys]].

==Playing career==
===Sydney Roosters / Newtown Jets===
Baitieri joined the [[Sydney Roosters]] via their feeder club, the [[Newtown Jets]]. He made his NRL debut against the [[Wests Tigers]] in May 2010.<ref name=family-affair>{{cite web|last=Lewis|first=Daniel|date=3 May 2010|title=Family affair has a French connection|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/family-affair-has-a-french-connection-20100502-u1l6.html|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|access-date=3 May 2010}}</ref>

===Catalans Dragons===
In August 2010, he joined the [[Catalans Dragons]] on a two-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|date=31 August 2010|title=Aussie scrum-half Jason Baitieri signs for Catalans|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/catalan/8958205.stm|publisher=BBC}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|last=Hadfield|first=Dave|date=1 September 2010|title=Dragons begin rebuilding with Baitieri|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/dragons-begin-rebuilding-with-baitieri-2067933.html|work=The Independent|location=London|access-date=25 March 2018}}</ref>

In July 2015, Baitieri extended his contract with Catalans for a further two years.<ref>{{cite web|date=3 July 2015|title=Jason Baitieri signs two-year extension with Catalans Dragons|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12216/9902151/jason-baitieri-signs-two-year-extension-with-catalans-dragons|publisher=Sky Sports|access-date=25 March 2018}}</ref>

He played in the [[2018 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/aug/25/catalans-dragons-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history|work=The Guardian|date=25 August 2018|access-date=5 November 2019}}</ref>

===Pia Donkeys===
On 11 Oct 2023 it was reported that he left [[Bradford Bulls]] at the end of the 2023 season and will be returning to France to play for [[Baroudeurs de Pia XIII|Pia Donkeys]] ready for the [[Elite One Championship 2023–2024]] season.<ref>{{cite web|website=Telegraph & Argus|date=11 Oct 2023|title=Jason Baitieri returns to France after Bradford Bulls stint|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/23849375.jason-baitieri-returns-france-bradford-bulls-stint/}}</ref>

==International career==
Baitieri made his international debut for France in the [[2010 European Cup]]. He also played in a friendly international against England in 2011, [[2012 Autumn International Series]] and he captained his country for the first time in their [[2014 European Cup]] campaign.

After missing France's first match of the [[2015 European Cup]] and test-match with England due to injury, Jason returned to captain France in their European Cup match against Wales.

He was selected in [[France national rugby league team|France 9s]] squad for the [[2019 Rugby League World Cup 9s]].<ref>{{cite web|date=2 October 2019|title=La liste des joueurs pour la Coupe du Monde dévoilée!|url=https://www.ffr13.fr/actualite-a-la-une/la-liste-des-joueurs-pour-la-coupe-du-monde-devoilee/|publisher=FFRXIII|access-date=1 November 2019|language=fr}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|date=10 October 2019|title=XIII de France: Romain Puso remplace Lilian Albert, forfait!|url=https://www.ffr13.fr/actualite-a-la-une/xiii-de-france-romain-puso-remplace-lilian-albert-forfait/|publisher=FFR XIII|access-date=1 November 2019|language=fr}}</ref>
